authority: repository-local documentation, skill-mirror, tooling, metadata, dependency, test, and route changes only; no provider or external-system access or mutation
acceptedOutcome: Bundjil has executable bounded docs and skill controls, complete required Claude mirrors, YAML/Schema-validated skill metadata, semantic current-owner claim checks, preserved occurrence-control equivalence, and positive/negative fixtures wired into verification.
candidateInputPathScope: All HGI-302 worktree changes before adding this candidate receipt; the set includes the audit-index route and excludes only this receipt.

checks.check: check:docs positive repository pass and negative fixtures
checks.result: The live check passed 204 selected files and emitted a bounded JSON report; fixtures reject metadata, links, indexes, lifecycle, commands, missing package READMEs, successor/tombstone gaps, personal paths, semantic paraphrases, accepted-task drift, and weakened occurrence controls.

checks.check: check:skills positive repository pass and negative fixtures
checks.result: The live check passed 164 instruction/reference files and all 15 Claude mirrors; fixtures reject broken, missing, and legacy mirrors, YAML/Schema metadata errors, broken references, personal paths, and contradictory executable examples across local skills.

The evaluator first rejected phrase-only contradiction logic and weak metadata/example coverage. Structured claim modelling, accepted-task comparison, YAML plus Effect Schema decoding, all-local-skill example scanning, and paraphrase fixtures then received PASS.
The accepted HGI-308 decision is mechanically required, and the real boundary suite preserves exact matching, duplicate rejection, occurrence isolation, and stale-entry detection.

Effect setup, boundary and skill/docs audits, 38 tooling tests, Ultracite, lint fixtures, Knip, five workspace typechecks, and 250 package/app tests passed with schema-valid synthetic Executor test configuration and no provider call.

limitations: Structural and semantic repository controls do not prove provider, deployment, workflow, credential, release, or application-runtime actuality.
Semantic claim classification is an explicit checked-in policy model; new claim domains or vocabulary require owner-reviewed rule and fixture updates.
The JSON policy reports are per-run ignored receipts; this candidate binds their observed digests and the durable closeout receipt will bind committed source identity.
nonClaims: No provider, deployment, workflow, GitHub setting, secret, webhook, message, credential, release, production, or publication system was read or changed.
The docs and skill checks do not grant operation authority or replace provider/runtime proof.
rollback: Revert the bounded HGI-302 semantic commit; preserve the accepted HGI-308 occurrence-control decision and prior HGI-300/HGI-301/HGI-308 receipts.
nextTrigger: Commit the accepted semantic candidate, create a content-addressed Git validation receipt, publish/read back main, and close HGI-302 before HGI-310.
